_schema.description: Coverage universe for Furton Coverage. Currently a CANDIDATE list (~15) for Merge Gate 1. Nick freezes the final ~10 there by setting candidate=false on the cuts; the survivors become the frozen roster (changes thereafter require a dated note per PLAN §5).

fields.cik: 10-digit zero-padded SEC CIK. Resolved by Session 1 from sec.gov/files/company_tickers.json on 2026-07-08. Every EDGAR call needs this.
fields.company: Legal name as it appears in the SEC ticker map.
fields.sector: Coverage sector bucket (for site grouping + spread).
fields.fiscal_year_end: Month the fiscal year ends (helps explain off-calendar reporting).
fields.typical_reporting_months: Months the company usually files its quarterly earnings 8-K (staggering signal).
fields.key_kpi_concepts: XBRL us-gaap/dei concept tags + company-specific KPIs we expect to model. '(KPI)' = a management metric that may NOT be XBRL-tagged and could live only in 8-K prose — Session 2 verifies actual tagging depth.
fields.dow_member: Best-effort flag (as of Jan-2026 knowledge). Drives the PLAN §2 policy: FINAL universe overlaps the Furton Research Dow roster by AT MOST 3. MERGE GATE MUST CONFIRM these against the live Dow 30.
fields.guidance_prior: Session 1's PRIOR on whether the company puts QUANTITATIVE forward guidance in its 8-K press release: written_strong | written_partial | verify. This is a hypothesis, NOT the ruling.
fields.guidance_status: AUTHORITATIVE guidable/call-only/no-guidance ruling. Set to 'pending_s2_validation' by Session 1; Session 2 assesses in notes/_inbox/edgar_report.md and the merge gate writes the final value here. Call-only names are a hard exclude unless deliberately accepted (PLAN Merge Gate 1).
fields.basis: gaap | non_gaap — the basis the company GUIDES on and the market reacts to. Prior from Session 1; confirmed at gate. Drives score.py's refuse-to-cross-bases rule (PLAN §3 GAAP trap).
fields.candidate: true = in the running for Merge Gate 1. Set false to cut. Frozen roster = the entries left true after the gate.
fields.one_liner: Why this name earns a coverage slot.

_schema.resolver: Session 1 (scaffold) — CIKs via sec.gov/files/company_tickers.json, User-Agent 'Furton Coverage nicholas@furton.com'.
_schema.dow_overlap_note: 4 candidates are flagged dow_member=true (NVDA, CRM, UNH, V). The final 10 may keep AT MOST 3 per PLAN §2 — the gate must cut at least one, and must re-confirm every dow_member flag against the current Dow 30 (roster shifts; e.g. GOOGL<->VZ swap 2026-06-29).

universe.one_liner: The reference KPI-rich, gives explicit next-quarter revenue + gross-margin + opex guidance in the release; enables a Furton Research cross-comparison (an allowed Dow overlap).
Off-calendar (Mar/Jun/Sep/Dec) memory bellwether — fills the reporting-month gaps the Dec-FY names all miss; guides next-quarter revenue, gross margin and EPS in the release.
Textbook written guidance (full-year + next-quarter revenue and EPS, plus cRPO and margin) — but Dow member, so competes against NVDA/UNH/V for the <=3 overlap slots.

Second off-calendar name (May FY) covering Mar/Jun/Sep/Dec; VERIFY guidance depth — Oracle gives a lot of next-quarter color verbally on the call, so Session 2 must confirm how much lands in the written release.
Cybersecurity representation with rich written guidance (full-year + next-quarter revenue, NGS ARR, billings, EPS); July FY staggers it off the crowded Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct cluster.

Guides next-quarter revenue, operating margin and EPS in the shareholder letter (filed as 8-K EX-99.1) — cleanly gradable; note it stopped guiding subscriber adds in 2025, so lean on the financial guidance.

key_kpi_concepts: total costs and expenses (KPI, guided as a range)
capital expenditures (KPI, guided as a range)
universe.one_liner: Guides next-quarter total-revenue range PLUS full-year total-expense and capex ranges in the release — an unusually gradable multi-line guide, and a mega-cap advertising read.

key_kpi_concepts: product revenue: Mounjaro / Zepbound / Verzenio (KPI, XBRL ProductMember dims)
universe.one_liner: Guides full-year revenue and both reported & non-GAAP EPS in the release, and tags product-level revenue in XBRL (GLP-1 franchise) — a deep model and a healthcare anchor.

key_kpi_concepts: installed base of da Vinci systems (KPI)
universe.one_liner: Guides full-year procedure growth, gross margin and opex growth in the release — a distinctive non-financial KPI (procedures) to grade; med-device diversification.
Guides full-year adjusted EPS and revenue with a re-affirm/raise cadence every quarter — very gradable; but Dow member, so counts against the <=3 overlap cap.

key_kpi_concepts: segment sales: Production & Precision Ag / Small Ag & Turf / Construction & Forestry
universe.one_liner: Guides full-year NET INCOME as a dollar range in the release (an unusually clean, GAAP-basis number to grade) — cyclical-industrial exposure and an Oct FY that staggers well.

GE Aerospace guides full-year adjusted EPS, free cash flow and revenue growth in the release — aerospace-cycle exposure; note the post-spin entity, so history pre-2024 needs care (Session 2 to check XBRL continuity).

key_kpi_concepts: comparable store sales growth (KPI, historically GUIDED)
universe.one_liner: Consumer/restaurants read with a distinctive comp-sales KPI — but VERIFY: Starbucks SUSPENDED formal guidance during its FY2025 turnaround, so Session 2 must confirm whether it currently guides in writing before this name is gradable.

key_kpi_concepts: segment revenue: Frito-Lay / PBNA / intl divisions
universe.one_liner: Guides full-year organic revenue growth and core constant-currency EPS in the release — defensive staples ballast, and a reliably re-affirmed/updated guide to grade.

Payments-network exposure with rich volume KPIs — but Dow member AND much of the outlook is call-driven; VERIFY how much of the net-revenue/EPS-growth guide is actually written before spending an overlap slot on it.
